For example, if there are existing subsequences that end with x-1, we can add the current number to one of them. Otherwise, we need to check if there are enough numbers to form a new subsequence.

For example, if there are existing subsequences ending with (current number - 1), then we can add this number to one of those, decrement the count for that ending, and increment the count for endings now at current number. If not, we check the next number. For instance, if there's a number that can start a new sequence of three consecutive numbers (current, current+1, current+2), then we create a subsequence ending at current+2, and adjust counts accordingly.

1. Use a frequency map (count) to track how many times each number is present.
2. Use a hash map (tails) to track how many subsequences end with a particular number.
3. Iterate through each number in the array:

   b. Else, check if there's a subsequence that can take this number as next element (i.e., tails has (current -1) with count > 0). If yes, decrease tails for (current -1) and increase for current.

   d. If neither is possible, return false.

So here's the code outline:

public boolean isPossible(int[] nums) {
    // build counts
    Map<Integer, Integer> counts = new HashMap<>();
    for (int num : nums) {
        counts.put(num, counts.getOrDefault(num, 0) + 1);
    }
    // build tails
    Map<Integer, Integer> tails = new HashMap<>();
    for (int num : nums) {
        if (counts.get(num) == 0) continue;
        // check if can add to existing subsequence
        if (tails.getOrDefault(num - 1, 0) > 0) {
            tails.put(num - 1, tails.get(num - 1) - 1);
            tails.put(num, tails.getOrDefault(num, 0) + 1);
            counts.put(num, counts.get(num) - 1);
        } else {
            // check if can form new subsequence
            if (counts.getOrDefault(num + 1, 0) > 0 && counts.getOrDefault(num + 2, 0) > 0) {
                tails.put(num + 2, tails.getOrDefault(num + 2, 0) + 1);
                counts.put(num, counts.get(num) - 1);
                counts.put(num + 1, counts.get(num + 1) - 1);
                counts.put(num + 2, counts.get(num + 2) - 1);
            } else {
                return false;
            }
        }
    }
    return true;
}

public boolean isPossible(int[] nums) {
    Map<Integer, Integer> counts = new HashMap<>();

public boolean isPossible(int[] nums) {
    // build counts
    Map<Integer, Integer> counts = new HashMap<>();
    for (int num : nums) {
        counts.put(num, counts.getOrDefault(num, 0) + 1);
    }
    // build tails
    Map<Integer, Integer> tails = new HashMap<>();
    for (int num : nums) {
        if (counts.get(num) == 0) continue;
        // check if can add to existing subsequence
        if (tails.getOrDefault(num - 1, 0) > 0) {
            tails.put(num - 1, tails.get(num - 1) - 1);
            tails.put(num, tails.getOrDefault(num, 0) + 1);
            counts.put(num, counts.get(num) - 1);
        } else {
            // check if can form new subsequence
            if (counts.getOrDefault(num + 1, 0) > 0 && counts.getOrDefault(num + 2, 0) > 0) {
                tails.put(num + 2, tails.getOrDefault(num + 2, 0) + 1);
                counts.put(num, counts.get(num) - 1);
                counts.put(num + 1, counts.get(num + 1) - 1);
                counts.put(num + 2, counts.get(num + 2) - 1);
            } else {
                return false;
            }
        }
    }
    return true;
}
